Standard Hounsfield Unit definition. Water at STP is defined as 0 HU by the Hounsfield scale definition.

Medical imaging standard for bone segmentation. Search results confirm 'Bone is typically +400 HU or greater'. Published research (Nature Scientific Reports, ScienceDirect) shows bone thresholds commonly range from 200-500 HU depending on application.

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 38-66 show primary download fails on HTTP 404, triggering synthetic phantom generation. The synthetic phantom is procedurally generated with known HU values (Air=-1000, Skull=1000, Brain=40, Ventricles=0).

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Hounsfield Unit calibration standards with verified reference values: air at -1000 HU, water at 0 HU (standard definitions), and bone threshold at 400 HU (standard clinical threshold for bone segmentation). File paths are correctly specified. The input data source is synthetic (procedurally generated phantom), not real medical data, because the referenced ImageJ ct.tif URL (https://imagej.nih.gov/ij/images/ct.tif) returns HTTP 404. The synthetic phantom is designed with known tissue regions: background air, circular skull, brain, and ventricles with appropriate HU values.
